title: RFC: Shard Cache Design
bodyMarkdown:
This RFC proposes the design behind src/checkpoint/shardCache.ts, which the loader uses to warm shard bytes ahead of restore.

## Problem
Before this design, the loader read every shard from disk on every warm call, even shards it had already fetched moments earlier during a retry. That wasted IO and slowed restore under any kind of transient failure.

## Design
A pinned in-memory cache keyed by shard key. Pinning keeps a shard resident until the loader explicitly releases it, so a shard mid-restore never gets evicted out from under an in-flight read. Unpinned entries fall back to least-recently-used eviction once the cache exceeds its byte budget, currently 8 GiB by default.

## Alternatives considered
A disk-backed cache was considered and rejected: it would have added another IO layer on the exact path we are trying to make faster. An unbounded in-memory cache was also rejected, it risks OOM-killing the loader process on large clusters.

## Status
Shipped. The eviction logic lives entirely in ShardCache, no other module reaches into its internals. Questions go to Priya Natarajan.
